stories.headline: 21 people killed and dozens injured in massive explosion at fireworks plant in China
stories.summary: Authorities are still investigating the reasons of the explosion, with negligence being suspected as a likely cause. Officials warned that the death toll is likely to climb as search and rescue operations continue.

stories.headline: Explosion at China fireworks factory kills 26 people
stories.summary: A blast at a fireworks factory in Hunan province has killed 26 people and injured 61, state media report.

stories.headline: An explosion at a fireworks plant in China has killed at least 21 people
stories.summary: An explosion at a fireworks plant in a central Chinese province has killed at least 21 people and injured 61 others.

stories.headline: Ships Cluster Further From Hormuz Strait as Iran Widens Control
stories.summary: Hundreds of vessels were seen clustering near Dubai on Tuesday, as more ships moved away from a still-empty Strait of Hormuz in response to Iran’s efforts to widen its area of control.

stories.headline: Iran says US military killed five civilians in attacks on passenger boats
stories.summary: Iranian commander says US military attacked two passenger boats, not IRGC vessels, in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday.

stories.headline: Shares slip as oil prices stay elevated near peaks on Iran war concerns
stories.summary: Asian stocks declined on Tuesday, tracking losses on Wall Street after US stocks retreated from record highs. Crude, meanwhile, remains firm.

stories.headline: Secret Service Shoots and Wounds Armed Man Near Washington Monument
stories.summary: A 15-year-old boy was also hit. The motorcade of Vice President JD Vance had passed through the area shortly before the shooting.

stories.summary: Two people have been confirmed dead while two others remain hospitalised and in critical condition after a man rammed a vehicle into a crowd of people in a busy shopping district in the eastern city of Leipzig.

stories.headline: Deadly China plane crash was caused by fuel cut-off, says report
stories.summary: The Boeing 737 plane crashed into a Chinese hillside in 2022, killing all 132 people on board.

stories.headline: BlackRock Touts ETFs as Liquidity Antidote to Private Exposure
stories.summary: Exchange-traded funds can be the source of liquidity that retail investors need after ramping up exposure to private assets, BlackRock Inc. executives wrote in a report.

stories.headline: Indian Rupee at Record Low as Analysts Dust Off 2013 RBI Playbook
stories.summary: The Indian rupee fell to a record low as crude prices rose following a flare-up in violence in the Middle East, leading analysts to consider whether the central bank may return to its 2013 playbook to support the currency.

stories.headline: Markets on Edge as US, Iran Trade Fire in Gulf | The Asia Trade 5/5/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yotta Data Said to Hire Advisors for $900 Million India IPO
stories.summary: Yotta Data Services Pvt. is weighing an initial public offering in Mumbai that could raise as much as $900 million and has engaged investment banks for the process, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Indonesia Intervenes in Markets as Rupiah Sets New Record Low
stories.summary: Indonesia’s central bank intervened in the foreign-exchange markets as the rupiah set a new record low.

stories.headline: Hormuz tensions push ceasefire to the brink as Trump threatens Iran
stories.summary: US military says it is encouraging ships to pass through Hormuz Strait, but Iran says it is in full control of waterway.

stories.headline: Alabbar: Dubai Property Market Resilient
stories.summary: Emaar Properties Founder Mohamed Alabbar says that Dubai’s property market remains resilient despite regional tensions, citing strong buyer confidence and stable collections across customers. While acknowledging short-term supply chain disruptions linked to the Strait of Hormuz closure, Alabbar expects a quick regional rebound. He spoke with Bloomberg TV’s Joumanna Bercetche from the sidelines of MIITE in Abu Dhabi.

stories.headline: Regis to Buy Vault Minerals to Form $7.7 Billion Gold Miner
stories.summary: Australian gold miner Regis Resources Ltd. has agreed to buy Vault Minerals Ltd. in an all-share transaction that they said values the combined entity at about A$10.7 billion ($7.7 billion) and will attract a wider range of shareholders.

stories.headline: Appeals Court Blocks Ex-Prisoner From Taking Office in New Orleans
stories.summary: Calvin Duncan arrived for his first day as the newly elected criminal court clerk, a job the state wants to eliminate. Then a federal court intervened.

stories.headline: Judge Questions Why Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Suspect Was Placed on Suicide Watch
stories.summary: A federal magistrate ordered that the D.C. Department of Corrections explain why it had placed Cole Tomas Allen in especially restrictive conditions.

stories.headline: Home on the Range No More: Trump Wants Bison Gone
stories.summary: The Trump administration is evicting bison herds from federal grasslands in Montana, siding with ranchers and Republican leaders over environmentalists and tribal leaders.

stories.headline: Pirro will not appeal judge’s quashing of Fed subpoenas
stories.summary: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro’s office has dropped plans to appeal a ruling by a federal judge that quashed two subpoenas to the Federal Reserve and its chairman.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.
